name: Apple HomePod
short_description: A Siri-powered smart speaker that listens for voice commands and controls Apple Music, HomeKit devices, and the home.
long_description: The HomePod is a stationary domestic speaker built around a far-field microphone array and the Siri voice assistant, using on-device sensing to tune its sound to the room and to hear wake-word commands across a space. As a piece of physical AI it shows how an appliance-shaped object becomes an ambient agent: it has no screen, face, or mobility, yet it sits in the home as an always-listening hub that mediates music, messaging, smart-home control, and household routines. Its form keeps it socially low-key while making AI a fixed, room-scale presence in daily life.

images.caption: An original Apple HomePod smart speaker, June 2018.
images.alt_text: A cylindrical Apple HomePod smart speaker covered in white mesh fabric, standing on a wooden surface.

sources.title: HomePod (1st generation) - Technical Specifications
sources.access_notes: Official Apple specs: six-microphone array, A8 chip, Siri.

sources.title: HomePod
sources.access_notes: Launch dates: original Feb 9 2018; 2nd generation Feb 3 2023.

interactions.title: Shout a message to the whole house
interactions.description: Say "Hey Siri, announce that dinner is ready" and your recorded voice plays out of every HomePod in the home and lands as a notification on everyone's iPhone and Apple Watch.

interactions.title: It hears the smoke alarm when you're out
interactions.description: Left running while you're away, the HomePod listens for the chirp of a smoke or carbon-monoxide detector and pings your phone; you can tap the alert to listen in on the room and talk to whoever is home.

interactions.title: Ask the speaker to find your phone
interactions.description: When your iPhone has vanished into the couch, say "Hey Siri, where's my iPhone?" and the HomePod sets it ringing until you dig it out.
interactions.source_label: iMore — find your iPhone with HomePod

interactions.title: Pour a song from your hand into the room
interactions.description: Walk in holding your iPhone, tap it to the top of the HomePod, and whatever you were listening to on headphones keeps playing out loud without missing a beat.

interactions.title: The speaker becomes a room thermometer
interactions.description: The built-in temperature and humidity sensor lets you build automations so the room's own readings close the blinds, kick on a fan, or run a dehumidifier when it gets too warm or muggy.

interactions.title: Kids learn to say "please" to it
interactions.description: Parents report their children copy how the family talks to the speaker, so many insist everyone add "please" and "thank you" when asking Siri — turning the always-listening cylinder into an accidental manners coach.
interactions.source_label: TODAY — saying please to smart speakers

interactions.title: A doorless intercom between bedrooms
interactions.description: A teenager upstairs taps Reply on an Intercom message instead of yelling down the stairwell, and a parent in the kitchen hears the answer come back through the speaker beside them.

interactions.title: A standing morning check-in
interactions.description: Walk past on the way out and ask for the day's weather, traffic, and first calendar event in one breath, the speaker reading it back while your hands stay full of keys and coffee.
